description: Detailed backend development tasks for Phases 0 (Infrastructure & Core Architecture) and 1 (Onboarding & Content Engine) of the LinguistAI AI-powered language learning platform. Built with FastAPI (full OOP, SOLID principles, no code comments), PostgreSQL via async SQLAlchemy, and Google Vertex AI through the google-genai SDK.

phases.name: Infrastructure & Core Architecture
phases.description: Establish the foundational project structure, database layer, authentication system, configuration management, and Vertex AI integration backbone. Every subsequent feature depends on this phase being rock-solid.

modules.name: Project Scaffolding
modules.description: Initialize the FastAPI project with a clean, SOLID-compliant directory layout, dependency management, base abstractions, custom exceptions, and structured logging.

tasks.title: Initialize FastAPI project with canonical directory structure
tasks.description: Create the top-level FastAPI application entry point and the full directory tree: api/ (routers and dependencies), services/ (business logic layer), repositories/ (data access layer), models/ (SQLAlchemy ORM models), schemas/ (Pydantic request/response schemas), core/ (config, security, exceptions, logging). Each package must contain an __init__.py that re-exports its public interface. The main application factory must wire up lifespan events, exception handlers, and the top-level APIRouter.
acceptance_criteria: Running `uvicorn app.main:app --reload` starts the server on port 8000 without errors
Directory tree contains api/, services/, repositories/, models/, schemas/, core/ packages with __init__.py files
Application factory function creates and returns a configured FastAPI instance with a lifespan context manager
A root GET / endpoint returns {"service": "LinguistAI", "status": "ok"}

tasks.title: Configure pyproject.toml with all project dependencies
tasks.description: Create pyproject.toml using the [project] table with all required runtime dependencies: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], sqlalchemy[asyncio], asyncpg, alembic, pydantic>=2.0, pydantic-settings, google-genai, google-cloud-texttospeech, google-cloud-speech, google-cloud-storage, python-jose[cryptography], passlib[bcrypt], httpx. Add optional dev dependency groups for pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx (test client), ruff, mypy. Pin minimum versions for critical packages.
acceptance_criteria: pyproject.toml is valid and parseable by pip/uv/poetry without errors
All 14 runtime dependencies are listed with minimum version constraints
A [project.optional-dependencies] dev group includes pytest, pytest-asyncio, ruff, and mypy
`pip install -e .` or `uv pip install -e .` installs the project and all dependencies successfully
A scripts entry or Makefile target exists for `dev` (uvicorn with reload) and `test` (pytest)

tasks.title: Create base abstract classes (AbstractRepository, AbstractService)
tasks.description: Define AbstractRepository as a generic abstract base class parameterized by model type and primary key type, with abstract methods: get_by_id, get_all, create, update, delete. Define AbstractService as an abstract base class that receives a repository instance via constructor injection. Both classes must enforce the contract through Python's abc.ABC and @abstractmethod. AbstractRepository should accept an AsyncSession via constructor to support unit-of-work patterns.
acceptance_criteria: AbstractRepository is generic over ModelType and PKType with all five CRUD abstract methods declared
AbstractRepository constructor accepts an AsyncSession parameter and stores it as a protected attribute
AbstractService constructor accepts an AbstractRepository instance and stores it as a protected attribute
Attempting to instantiate either abstract class directly raises TypeError
A concrete subclass that omits any abstract method raises TypeError at instantiation

tasks.title: Create custom exception hierarchy
tasks.description: Implement a layered exception hierarchy in core/exceptions.py. Base: AppException with status_code, detail, and error_code fields. Subclasses: NotFoundException (404), ConflictException (409), UnauthorizedException (401), ForbiddenException (403), ValidationException (422), ExternalServiceException (502), RateLimitException (429). Register a global exception handler on the FastAPI app that catches AppException subclasses and returns a consistent JSON error envelope: {"error": {"code": str, "message": str, "details": Any | null}}.
acceptance_criteria: AppException base class carries status_code, detail, and error_code attributes with sensible defaults
At least seven concrete exception subclasses exist with correct HTTP status codes
Global exception handler is registered on the FastAPI app and returns the specified JSON envelope
Raising NotFoundException inside a route returns 404 with the error envelope format
Unhandled Python exceptions are caught by a fallback handler returning 500 with a generic error envelope

tasks.title: Configure structured logging
tasks.description: Set up structured JSON logging using Python's built-in logging module with a custom JSON formatter. Create a LoggerFactory in core/logging.py that produces named loggers with consistent formatting. Log entries must include timestamp (ISO 8601), level, logger name, message, and optional extra fields (request_id, user_id). Integrate a middleware that assigns a unique request_id (UUID4) to each incoming request and attaches it to the log context via contextvars. In development mode, output human-readable colored logs; in production, output JSON lines.
acceptance_criteria: LoggerFactory.get_logger(name) returns a configured Logger instance with the JSON formatter attached
Every HTTP request generates an access log entry containing method, path, status_code, duration_ms, and request_id
request_id is propagated to all log entries emitted during the same request lifecycle via contextvars
Setting APP_ENV=development switches output to human-readable colored format
Setting APP_ENV=production outputs one JSON object per line to stdout

modules.name: Database Foundation
modules.description: Configure the async SQLAlchemy engine, session management, declarative base model, Alembic migrations, and a health check endpoint to verify database connectivity.

tasks.title: Configure SQLAlchemy async engine and session factory
tasks.description: Create a DatabaseManager class in core/database.py that initializes an async SQLAlchemy engine (create_async_engine) from the DATABASE_URL setting. The manager must expose an async_session_factory (async_sessionmaker bound to the engine) and provide an async context manager get_session() that yields an AsyncSession with automatic commit-on-success and rollback-on-error semantics. Register a FastAPI dependency that calls get_session() so routes can inject AsyncSession.
acceptance_criteria: DatabaseManager.engine is created with create_async_engine using the configured DATABASE_URL
DatabaseManager.get_session() is an async context manager that yields AsyncSession, commits on clean exit, and rolls back on exception
A FastAPI Depends callable is available that provides an AsyncSession per request
Engine creation uses pool_size, max_overflow, and pool_timeout parameters from configuration
DatabaseManager exposes an async close() method that disposes of the engine and is called during app shutdown

tasks.title: Create base SQLAlchemy declarative model
tasks.description: Define a Base declarative model using SQLAlchemy 2.0's DeclarativeBase. Create a TimestampMixin that adds created_at (server_default=now()) and updated_at (onupdate=now()) columns. Create an IDMixin that provides a UUID primary key column with server-side default. All future models will inherit from Base and include both mixins. The Base.metadata must set a naming convention for constraints (ix_, uq_, ck_, fk_, pk_) to ensure Alembic auto-generates deterministic migration names.
acceptance_criteria: Base class extends DeclarativeBase and is importable from models/base.py
TimestampMixin adds created_at (DateTime with timezone, server_default) and updated_at (DateTime with timezone, onupdate) columns
IDMixin adds an id column of type UUID with server_default=gen_random_uuid() as the primary key
Base.metadata.naming_convention is set with patterns for ix, uq, ck, fk, and pk
A trivial test model inheriting Base + both mixins produces a valid CREATE TABLE statement

tasks.title: Implement database connection manager with connection pooling
tasks.description: Extend DatabaseManager to support configurable connection pooling: pool_size (default 10), max_overflow (default 20), pool_timeout (default 30s), pool_recycle (default 1800s), pool_pre_ping (True). Add an async method check_connection() that executes SELECT 1 and returns True/False. Add a method get_pool_status() that returns current pool size, checked-out connections, and overflow count for monitoring. Wire pool settings from the Pydantic Settings class.
acceptance_criteria: Engine is created with pool_size, max_overflow, pool_timeout, pool_recycle, and pool_pre_ping from config
check_connection() executes a lightweight query and returns a boolean without raising exceptions
get_pool_status() returns a dict with keys: pool_size, checked_out, overflow, checkedin
Connection pool parameters are configurable via environment variables through Pydantic Settings
Pool recycle is set to prevent stale connections from causing OperationalError on long-idle deployments

tasks.title: Create Alembic configuration and initial migration scaffold
tasks.description: Initialize Alembic with `alembic init` and configure alembic.ini and env.py for async operation. env.py must import Base.metadata and use run_async to run migrations. Configure the sqlalchemy.url to be read from the same DATABASE_URL environment variable used by the app. Create an initial empty migration (revision) as a baseline. Add a Makefile target or script: `make migrate` (alembic upgrade head), `make migration msg=...` (alembic revision --autogenerate -m ...).
acceptance_criteria: alembic.ini and migrations/ directory exist at the project root
env.py uses async engine and imports target_metadata from the application Base
Running `alembic upgrade head` on an empty database succeeds without errors
Running `alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'test'` detects model changes and generates a migration file
Database URL in Alembic is sourced from the same environment variable as the application

tasks.title: Implement health check endpoint
tasks.description: Create a /health API endpoint that returns the overall system health. The endpoint must check database connectivity via DatabaseManager.check_connection() and return a structured response: {"status": "healthy|degraded|unhealthy", "checks": {"database": {"status": "up|down", "latency_ms": float}, "timestamp": str}}. If the database is down, return HTTP 503 with status=unhealthy. This endpoint must not require authentication.
acceptance_criteria: GET /health returns 200 with status=healthy when the database is reachable
GET /health returns 503 with status=unhealthy when the database is unreachable
Response includes database check latency in milliseconds
Response includes an ISO 8601 timestamp
The endpoint is excluded from authentication middleware

modules.name: Authentication System
modules.description: Full JWT-based authentication flow: User model, password hashing, token issuance/refresh, auth repository, auth service, auth API routes, and a FastAPI dependency for extracting the current authenticated user.

tasks.title: Implement User SQLAlchemy model
tasks.description: Create a User model inheriting from Base with IDMixin and TimestampMixin. Columns: email (String, unique, indexed, not null), hashed_password (String, not null), full_name (String, nullable), is_active (Boolean, default True), is_superuser (Boolean, default False), last_login_at (DateTime, nullable). Add a unique constraint on email. Generate an Alembic migration for this model.
acceptance_criteria: User model is defined in models/user.py and inherits Base, IDMixin, TimestampMixin
email column has a unique index and is non-nullable
hashed_password column is a non-nullable String of sufficient length (at least 255)
An Alembic migration file is generated that creates the users table with all columns and constraints
Running `alembic upgrade head` creates the users table in the database

tasks.title: Implement password hashing service
tasks.description: Create a PasswordService class in services/password_service.py that wraps passlib's CryptContext with bcrypt as the primary scheme and auto-deprecated support for argon2. Methods: hash_password(plain: str) -> str and verify_password(plain: str, hashed: str) -> bool. The service must be stateless and instantiated once at app startup. CryptContext must use bcrypt with a configurable rounds parameter (default 12).
acceptance_criteria: PasswordService.hash_password returns a bcrypt hash string starting with $2b$
PasswordService.verify_password returns True for a correct plain/hash pair and False otherwise
Hashing the same plaintext twice produces different hashes (salt verification)
The bcrypt rounds parameter is configurable via the Settings class
The service is instantiated as a singleton and injectable via FastAPI dependency injection

tasks.title: Implement JWT token service
tasks.description: Create a TokenService class in services/token_service.py. Methods: create_access_token(subject: str, extra_claims: dict | None) -> str, create_refresh_token(subject: str) -> str, decode_token(token: str) -> TokenPayload. Access tokens expire after a configurable ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES (default 30). Refresh tokens expire after REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS (default 7). Tokens are signed with HS256 using JWT_SECRET_KEY from config. TokenPayload is a Pydantic model with sub, exp, iat, jti (UUID), and type (access|refresh) fields.
acceptance_criteria: create_access_token produces a JWT with sub, exp, iat, jti, and type=access claims
create_refresh_token produces a JWT with type=refresh and a longer expiration
decode_token validates the signature, checks expiration, and returns a TokenPayload Pydantic model
Expired tokens raise UnauthorizedException with error_code=TOKEN_EXPIRED
Tokens with invalid signatures raise UnauthorizedException with error_code=TOKEN_INVALID

tasks.title: Create auth repository (CRUD for users)
tasks.description: Create AuthRepository extending AbstractRepository[User, UUID]. Implement all abstract CRUD methods plus: get_by_email(email: str) -> User | None, exists_by_email(email: str) -> bool, update_last_login(user_id: UUID) -> None. All queries must use async SQLAlchemy select() statements. The repository must not contain any business logic — only data access.
acceptance_criteria: AuthRepository extends AbstractRepository[User, UUID] and implements all five abstract methods
get_by_email executes a SELECT query filtered by email and returns User or None
exists_by_email returns a boolean without fetching the full model (uses exists() subquery)
update_last_login sets last_login_at to utcnow and commits within the session
All methods use async/await and operate on the injected AsyncSession

tasks.title: Create auth service (register, login, refresh, get_current_user)
tasks.description: Create AuthService extending AbstractService. Constructor receives AuthRepository, PasswordService, and TokenService. Methods: register(schema: RegisterRequest) -> AuthResponse, login(schema: LoginRequest) -> AuthResponse, refresh(refresh_token: str) -> AuthResponse, get_current_user(token: str) -> User. register must check for existing email (raise ConflictException), hash password, create user, and return tokens. login must verify credentials (raise UnauthorizedException on failure), update last_login, and return tokens. AuthResponse is a Pydantic schema with access_token, refresh_token, token_type, and user fields.
acceptance_criteria: register raises ConflictException if the email already exists
register hashes the password, creates the user, and returns AuthResponse with both tokens
login raises UnauthorizedException if the email is not found or password is incorrect
login updates last_login_at on successful authentication
refresh decodes the refresh token, validates type=refresh, and returns new access + refresh token pair

tasks.title: Create auth API router
tasks.description: Create an APIRouter in api/routers/auth.py with prefix /auth and tag 'Authentication'. Endpoints: POST /auth/register (accepts RegisterRequest, returns AuthResponse, 201), POST /auth/login (accepts LoginRequest, returns AuthResponse, 200), POST /auth/refresh (accepts RefreshRequest with refresh_token field, returns AuthResponse, 200), GET /auth/me (requires authentication, returns UserResponse, 200). All endpoints must have proper response_model declarations and OpenAPI summary/description. Wire the router into the main app.
acceptance_criteria: POST /auth/register creates a user and returns 201 with AuthResponse body
POST /auth/login with valid credentials returns 200 with AuthResponse body
POST /auth/refresh with a valid refresh token returns 200 with new token pair
GET /auth/me with a valid Bearer token returns 200 with UserResponse body
All endpoints appear in the OpenAPI docs at /docs with correct schemas

tasks.title: Implement FastAPI dependency for current user extraction
tasks.description: Create a get_current_user dependency in api/dependencies/auth.py that extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header using FastAPI's OAuth2PasswordBearer, decodes it via TokenService, fetches the User from AuthRepository, and returns the User object. Create a get_current_active_user dependency that wraps get_current_user and raises ForbiddenException if is_active is False. Both dependencies must be reusable via Depends().
acceptance_criteria: get_current_user extracts the token from the Authorization: Bearer <token> header
Missing or malformed Authorization header raises UnauthorizedException (401)
Expired or invalid token raises UnauthorizedException with appropriate error_code
get_current_active_user raises ForbiddenException (403) if user.is_active is False
Injecting Depends(get_current_active_user) into a route correctly provides the User object

modules.name: Configuration & Environment
modules.description: Centralized configuration via Pydantic Settings, environment-specific loading, rate limiting middleware, and CORS configuration.

tasks.title: Create Pydantic Settings class for all configuration
tasks.description: Create a Settings class in core/config.py extending pydantic_settings.BaseSettings. Fields grouped by concern: Database (DATABASE_URL, DB_POOL_SIZE, DB_MAX_OVERFLOW, DB_POOL_TIMEOUT, DB_POOL_RECYCLE), Vertex AI (VERTEX_AI_PROJECT, VERTEX_AI_LOCATION, VERTEX_AI_MODEL), JWT (JWT_SECRET_KEY, ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES, REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS), Rate Limiting (RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS), App (APP_ENV, APP_DEBUG, APP_NAME, APP_VERSION, CORS_ORIGINS). Use model_config with env_file='.env' and case_sensitive=True. Implement a get_settings() function with @lru_cache for singleton behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Settings class has all specified fields with appropriate types and default values
Settings loads values from a .env file when present via model_config
Environment variables override .env file values
get_settings() returns the same Settings instance on repeated calls (cached)
Missing required fields (DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET_KEY, VERTEX_AI_PROJECT) raise a ValidationError at startup

tasks.title: Implement environment-specific config loading
tasks.description: Extend Settings to support environment-specific .env files. When APP_ENV=development, load .env and .env.development; when APP_ENV=production, load .env and .env.production; when APP_ENV=test, load .env and .env.test. Create a .env.example template file documenting all required and optional variables with placeholder values. Add the actual .env files to .gitignore.
acceptance_criteria: Setting APP_ENV=production causes .env.production to be loaded as an overlay on .env
Setting APP_ENV=test causes .env.test to be loaded, enabling test-specific database URLs
.env.example exists with placeholder values for every configuration variable
.env, .env.development, .env.production, and .env.test are listed in .gitignore
If the environment-specific file does not exist, the app starts normally with just .env values

tasks.title: Create rate limiting middleware
tasks.description: Implement a RateLimitMiddleware as a Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware. Use an in-memory sliding window strategy keyed by client IP (with X-Forwarded-For support for proxied requests). Configurable via RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS (default 100) and RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS (default 60). When the limit is exceeded, raise RateLimitException (429) with a Retry-After header. Exempt /health and /docs endpoints from rate limiting. Log rate limit violations at WARNING level.
acceptance_criteria: Requests exceeding the configured limit within the window receive HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header
Rate limiting is keyed by client IP, correctly reading X-Forwarded-For when behind a reverse proxy
GET /health and GET /docs are exempt from rate limiting
Rate limit parameters are sourced from the Settings class
Each rate limit violation is logged at WARNING level with the client IP and current count

tasks.title: Create CORS middleware configuration
tasks.description: Configure FastAPI's CORSMiddleware using the CORS_ORIGINS list from Settings. In development, allow all origins ("*"); in production, restrict to the configured list. Allow methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS. Allow headers: Authorization, Content-Type, X-Request-ID. Expose headers: X-Request-ID, X-Rate-Limit-Remaining. Allow credentials in production mode when origins are explicitly listed.
acceptance_criteria: In development (APP_ENV=development), Access-Control-Allow-Origin is set to *
In production, Access-Control-Allow-Origin matches only the configured CORS_ORIGINS list
Preflight OPTIONS requests return correct CORS headers for allowed methods and headers
X-Request-ID is included in Access-Control-Expose-Headers
allow_credentials is True only when origins are explicitly configured (not wildcard)

modules.name: Vertex AI Integration Foundation
modules.description: Abstract AI provider interface, concrete Vertex AI implementation using the google-genai SDK, AI service factory, prompt management, and GCP media services (TTS, STT, Cloud Storage).

tasks.title: Create abstract AIProvider base class
tasks.description: Define an AbstractAIProvider ABC in services/ai/base.py with abstract methods: generate_content(prompt: str, system_instruction: str | None, config: GenerationConfig | None) -> str, generate_content_stream(prompt: str, system_instruction: str | None, config: GenerationConfig | None) -> AsyncIterator[str], generate_structured(prompt: str, response_schema: type[BaseModel], system_instruction: str | None, config: GenerationConfig | None) -> BaseModel. Define GenerationConfig as a Pydantic model with temperature, top_p, top_k, max_output_tokens, and response_mime_type fields.
acceptance_criteria: AbstractAIProvider is an ABC with three abstract async methods matching the specified signatures
GenerationConfig is a Pydantic model with temperature (float), top_p (float), top_k (int), max_output_tokens (int), response_mime_type (str) fields with sensible defaults
generate_structured return type is generic over the provided Pydantic response_schema class
Direct instantiation of AbstractAIProvider raises TypeError
All methods accept an optional system_instruction parameter for system-level prompting

tasks.title: Implement VertexAIProvider using google-genai SDK
tasks.description: Create VertexAIProvider extending AbstractAIProvider in services/ai/vertex_provider.py. Initialize the google.genai.Client with vertexai=True, project=settings.VERTEX_AI_PROJECT, location=settings.VERTEX_AI_LOCATION. Implement generate_content using client.models.generate_content() with the configured model name. Implement generate_content_stream using client.models.generate_content_stream() yielding text chunks. Implement generate_structured by passing response_schema to generate_content with response_mime_type='application/json' and parsing the result. Wrap all API calls in try/except to catch google.api_core.exceptions and re-raise as ExternalServiceException.
acceptance_criteria: Client is initialized with vertexai=True, project, and location from Settings
generate_content calls client.models.generate_content with model name, contents, and config
generate_content_stream yields string chunks from the streaming response asynchronously
generate_structured passes the Pydantic model as response_schema and returns a validated instance
Google API errors are caught and re-raised as ExternalServiceException with the original error detail

tasks.title: Create AI service factory
tasks.description: Implement an AIProviderFactory class in services/ai/factory.py that creates and returns the appropriate AIProvider based on configuration. The factory must support a provider registry (dict mapping provider names to classes) and a create(provider_name: str) -> AbstractAIProvider method. Default provider is 'vertex'. The factory should be a singleton and cache created provider instances. Register the factory as a FastAPI dependency so services can inject AbstractAIProvider.
acceptance_criteria: AIProviderFactory.create('vertex') returns a VertexAIProvider instance
Calling create() twice with the same provider name returns the same cached instance
Requesting an unknown provider name raises ValidationException with available options listed
A FastAPI dependency get_ai_provider() returns the default provider via the factory
The factory is extensible — adding a new provider requires only registering a class, no factory code changes

tasks.title: Implement prompt template management system
tasks.description: Create a PromptManager class in services/ai/prompts.py that loads, stores, and renders prompt templates. Templates are stored as .txt or .md files in a prompts/ directory, organized by feature (prompts/onboarding/, prompts/lessons/, etc.). Each template uses Python's string.Template or Jinja2-style placeholders for variable substitution. PromptManager.render(template_name: str, **variables) -> str loads the template file, substitutes variables, and returns the final prompt. Templates are cached in memory after first load.
acceptance_criteria: PromptManager.render('onboarding/placement_question', level='A2', language='English') returns a filled prompt string
Templates are loaded from the filesystem prompts/ directory relative to the project root
Templates are cached in memory after first load — subsequent renders of the same template skip file I/O
Requesting a non-existent template name raises NotFoundException with the template path in the message
Variable substitution handles missing variables gracefully by raising ValidationException listing the missing keys

tasks.title: Create TTS service class
tasks.description: Create a TextToSpeechService class in services/media/tts_service.py wrapping google-cloud-texttospeech. Methods: synthesize(text: str, language_code: str, voice_name: str | None, speaking_rate: float) -> bytes (returns MP3 audio bytes), synthesize_and_store(text: str, language_code: str, storage_path: str) -> str (synthesizes, uploads to Cloud Storage, returns public URL). Support SSML input for advanced prosody control. Default voice selection strategy: pick a WaveNet voice for the given language code. Handle API errors with ExternalServiceException.
acceptance_criteria: synthesize returns MP3 audio bytes for a given text and language code
synthesize_and_store uploads the audio to Cloud Storage and returns a signed URL or public URL
Voice selection defaults to a WaveNet voice for the specified language_code
SSML input is detected (starts with <speak>) and processed using the SSML input type
Google Cloud API errors are caught and wrapped in ExternalServiceException

tasks.title: Create STT service class
tasks.description: Create a SpeechToTextService class in services/media/stt_service.py wrapping google-cloud-speech. Methods: transcribe(audio_bytes: bytes, language_code: str, sample_rate: int) -> TranscriptionResult, transcribe_from_uri(gcs_uri: str, language_code: str) -> TranscriptionResult. TranscriptionResult is a Pydantic model with transcript (str), confidence (float), word_timestamps (list of WordInfo). Support both synchronous recognition (short audio <1 min) and async recognition (long audio). Handle API errors with ExternalServiceException.
acceptance_criteria: transcribe accepts raw audio bytes and returns a TranscriptionResult with transcript and confidence
transcribe_from_uri accepts a GCS URI and returns a TranscriptionResult
Audio shorter than 60 seconds uses synchronous recognition; longer audio uses async recognition
TranscriptionResult includes word-level timestamps when available
Google Cloud API errors are caught and wrapped in ExternalServiceException

tasks.title: Create Cloud Storage service for audio caching
tasks.description: Create a StorageService class in services/media/storage_service.py wrapping google-cloud-storage. Methods: upload(data: bytes, destination_path: str, content_type: str) -> str (returns GCS URI), download(source_path: str) -> bytes, get_signed_url(path: str, expiration_minutes: int) -> str, exists(path: str) -> bool, delete(path: str) -> None. The bucket name is sourced from Settings (GCS_BUCKET_NAME). Implement a content-hash-based naming strategy for deduplication: hash the audio content to generate the storage key so identical content is stored only once.
acceptance_criteria: upload stores bytes in the configured GCS bucket and returns the gs:// URI
download retrieves bytes from GCS by path
get_signed_url generates a time-limited signed URL for the specified object
exists returns True if the object exists in the bucket, False otherwise
Content-hash naming generates a deterministic key from the file content, preventing duplicate storage

phases.name: Onboarding & Content Engine
phases.description: Build the multi-language architecture, user profile and onboarding flow with AI-powered placement testing, and the core AI lesson generation pipeline with personalization, caching, TTS integration, and progress tracking.

modules.name: Multi-Language Architecture
modules.description: Data model and service layer for supporting multiple target languages, starting with English, and the CEFR proficiency level framework.

tasks.title: Create Language SQLAlchemy model
tasks.description: Define a Language model inheriting from Base with IDMixin and TimestampMixin. Columns: code (String(10), unique, indexed, not null — e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr'), name (String(100), not null — e.g., 'English'), native_name (String(100), nullable — e.g., 'English'), is_active (Boolean, default True), icon_url (String, nullable). Add a unique constraint on code. Generate an Alembic migration.
acceptance_criteria: Language model is defined in models/language.py with all specified columns
code column has a unique index and is limited to 10 characters
An Alembic migration creates the languages table with all columns and constraints
The model inherits IDMixin (UUID pk) and TimestampMixin (created_at, updated_at)
The model is importable from models/__init__.py

tasks.title: Create languages repository
tasks.description: Create LanguageRepository extending AbstractRepository[Language, UUID]. Implement all abstract CRUD methods plus: get_by_code(code: str) -> Language | None, get_active_languages() -> list[Language], exists_by_code(code: str) -> bool. All queries must be async.
acceptance_criteria: LanguageRepository extends AbstractRepository[Language, UUID] and implements all abstract methods
get_by_code returns a Language or None filtered by the code column
get_active_languages returns only languages where is_active is True, ordered by name
exists_by_code returns a boolean without loading the full model
All methods operate on the injected AsyncSession with async/await

tasks.title: Create seed migration for English language entry
tasks.description: Create a data migration (not schema migration) using Alembic's op.bulk_insert or op.execute to seed the languages table with the English language entry: code='en', name='English', native_name='English', is_active=True. The migration must be idempotent — running it twice must not create duplicate entries. Add additional seed entries for Spanish (es), French (fr), German (de), and Japanese (ja) with is_active=False as placeholders for future expansion.
acceptance_criteria: Running `alembic upgrade head` inserts the English language entry with is_active=True
Spanish, French, German, and Japanese entries are inserted with is_active=False
Running the migration twice does not create duplicate entries (idempotent via INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or equivalent)
The migration is a separate revision file from the schema migration
Downgrade removes all five seed entries

tasks.title: Create CEFR level enum
tasks.description: Define a CEFRLevel Python enum in models/enums.py with values: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2. Each value must map to a PostgreSQL enum type. Add a description property that returns human-readable labels (e.g., A1='Beginner', B1='Intermediate', C1='Advanced'). Add a class method from_score(score: float) -> CEFRLevel that maps a 0.0–1.0 placement test score to the appropriate level. Create a reusable SQLAlchemy column type for this enum.
acceptance_criteria: CEFRLevel enum has six values: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2
Each value has a description property returning a human-readable label
from_score(0.0) returns A1, from_score(1.0) returns C2, with proportional intermediate mappings
The enum is usable as a SQLAlchemy column type that maps to a PostgreSQL ENUM
An Alembic migration creates the cefr_level PostgreSQL enum type

tasks.title: Implement language service
tasks.description: Create LanguageService extending AbstractService with LanguageRepository. Methods: get_all_active() -> list[LanguageResponse], get_by_code(code: str) -> LanguageResponse (raises NotFoundException if not found or inactive), validate_language_code(code: str) -> Language (raises ValidationException if code is invalid or language is inactive). LanguageResponse is a Pydantic schema with id, code, name, native_name, and icon_url fields.
acceptance_criteria: get_all_active returns a list of LanguageResponse for all active languages
get_by_code returns a LanguageResponse or raises NotFoundException with the invalid code in the message
validate_language_code returns the Language ORM instance or raises ValidationException
LanguageResponse schema correctly serializes UUID id and all string fields
The service is injectable via FastAPI dependency injection

modules.name: User Profile & Onboarding
modules.description: User profile and goals models, the onboarding API flow, and the AI-driven adaptive placement test that determines the user's initial CEFR level.

tasks.title: Create UserProfile SQLAlchemy model
tasks.description: Define a UserProfile model inheriting from Base with IDMixin and TimestampMixin. Columns: user_id (ForeignKey to users.id, unique, indexed, not null), target_language_id (ForeignKey to languages.id, not null), native_language_code (String(10), not null), current_level (Enum CEFRLevel, nullable — set after placement test), placement_score (Float, nullable), daily_goal_minutes (Integer, default 15), streak_count (Integer, default 0), total_xp (Integer, default 0), onboarding_completed (Boolean, default False). Add relationships to User and Language. Generate an Alembic migration.
acceptance_criteria: UserProfile has a one-to-one relationship with User via user_id foreign key with unique constraint
target_language_id is a foreign key to languages.id with a many-to-one relationship
current_level uses the CEFRLevel PostgreSQL enum type
An Alembic migration creates the user_profiles table with all columns, constraints, and foreign keys
Deleting a User cascades to delete the associated UserProfile

tasks.title: Create UserGoals model
tasks.description: Define a UserGoals model inheriting from Base with IDMixin and TimestampMixin. Columns: user_id (ForeignKey to users.id, indexed, not null), goal_type (String(50), not null — e.g., 'travel', 'business', 'academic', 'casual'), description (Text, nullable), is_primary (Boolean, default False), priority_order (Integer, default 0). A user can have multiple goals. Add a relationship to User. Generate an Alembic migration. Add a unique constraint on (user_id, goal_type) to prevent duplicate goal types per user.
acceptance_criteria: UserGoals has a many-to-one relationship with User via user_id
A unique constraint on (user_id, goal_type) prevents duplicate goal types
goal_type is a non-nullable String(50)
An Alembic migration creates the user_goals table with all columns and constraints
The User model has a one-to-many relationship back to UserGoals

tasks.title: Implement profile repository
tasks.description: Create ProfileRepository extending AbstractRepository[UserProfile, UUID]. Implement all abstract CRUD methods plus: get_by_user_id(user_id: UUID) -> UserProfile | None, exists_for_user(user_id: UUID) -> bool, update_level(user_id: UUID, level: CEFRLevel, score: float) -> None, update_streak(user_id: UUID, increment: int) -> None, add_xp(user_id: UUID, xp: int) -> None. Include eager loading of the Language relationship.
acceptance_criteria: get_by_user_id returns UserProfile with the Language relationship eagerly loaded
exists_for_user returns a boolean using an exists() subquery
update_level sets current_level, placement_score, and onboarding_completed=True atomically
update_streak increments streak_count by the given value
add_xp increments total_xp by the given value using an atomic UPDATE

tasks.title: Implement goals repository
tasks.description: Create GoalsRepository extending AbstractRepository[UserGoals, UUID]. Implement all abstract CRUD methods plus: get_by_user_id(user_id: UUID) -> list[UserGoals], get_primary_goal(user_id: UUID) -> UserGoals | None, set_primary_goal(user_id: UUID, goal_id: UUID) -> None (unsets previous primary), delete_all_for_user(user_id: UUID) -> int (returns count deleted).
acceptance_criteria: get_by_user_id returns all goals for the user ordered by priority_order
get_primary_goal returns the goal with is_primary=True or None if no primary is set
set_primary_goal sets is_primary=False on all user goals, then True on the specified goal_id
delete_all_for_user removes all goals for the user and returns the count
All methods use async/await and operate on the injected AsyncSession

tasks.title: Create profile service with placement method logic
tasks.description: Create ProfileService extending AbstractService. Constructor receives ProfileRepository, GoalsRepository, and LanguageService. Methods: setup_profile(user_id: UUID, schema: ProfileSetupRequest) -> ProfileResponse (creates profile, validates language code, sets goals), get_profile(user_id: UUID) -> ProfileResponse (raises NotFoundException), update_goals(user_id: UUID, schema: GoalsUpdateRequest) -> list[GoalResponse], complete_placement(user_id: UUID, level: CEFRLevel, score: float) -> ProfileResponse. ProfileSetupRequest includes target_language_code, native_language_code, daily_goal_minutes, and goals list. Raise ConflictException if profile already exists.
acceptance_criteria: setup_profile validates the target_language_code via LanguageService before creating the profile
setup_profile raises ConflictException if a profile already exists for the user
setup_profile creates both the UserProfile and associated UserGoals in a single transaction
get_profile raises NotFoundException if no profile exists for the user_id
complete_placement updates current_level, placement_score, and sets onboarding_completed=True

tasks.title: Create onboarding API router
tasks.description: Create an APIRouter in api/routers/onboarding.py with prefix /profile and tag 'Onboarding'. Endpoints: POST /profile/setup (accepts ProfileSetupRequest, returns ProfileResponse, 201, requires auth), PUT /profile/goals (accepts GoalsUpdateRequest, returns list[GoalResponse], 200, requires auth), GET /profile (returns ProfileResponse, 200, requires auth), POST /profile/placement/start (starts placement test, returns first question), POST /profile/placement/answer (submits answer, returns next question or final result), GET /profile/placement/result (returns placement result if completed).
acceptance_criteria: POST /profile/setup creates a profile and returns 201 with ProfileResponse
PUT /profile/goals updates goals and returns 200 with updated goal list
GET /profile returns 200 with the current user profile or 404 if not set up
POST /profile/placement/start returns the first placement test question
All endpoints require Bearer token authentication via get_current_active_user dependency

tasks.title: Implement placement test question generation via Vertex AI
tasks.description: Create a PlacementTestService class that uses VertexAIProvider to generate adaptive placement test questions. The service maintains a placement session (stored in-memory or in the database) tracking: current question index, questions asked, answers given, running difficulty estimate. generate_question(session: PlacementSession) -> PlacementQuestion: generates a contextual question at the current estimated difficulty using a prompt template. PlacementQuestion is a Pydantic schema with: question_text, options (list of 4 choices), correct_answer_index, difficulty_level (A1-C2), explanation. The prompt instructs Vertex AI to generate the question in the target language with options and explanation, using generate_structured with PlacementQuestion as the response_schema.
acceptance_criteria: generate_question calls VertexAIProvider.generate_structured with PlacementQuestion as the response schema
The prompt includes the current difficulty estimate and target language
PlacementQuestion contains exactly 4 options, a correct_answer_index, and an explanation
Questions are generated at the appropriate CEFR level matching the current difficulty estimate
ExternalServiceException is raised if Vertex AI fails, with a user-friendly fallback message

tasks.title: Implement adaptive test scoring
tasks.description: Implement adaptive difficulty adjustment within PlacementTestService. Method: process_answer(session: PlacementSession, answer_index: int) -> PlacementStepResult. Logic: if the answer is correct, increase the difficulty estimate (move toward the next CEFR level); if incorrect, decrease it. Use a binary-search-inspired algorithm: start at B1 (midpoint), correct answers push toward B2/C1/C2, incorrect answers push toward A2/A1. After a configurable number of questions (default 10), or when the difficulty estimate stabilizes (two consecutive levels unchanged), end the test. PlacementStepResult includes: is_correct, explanation, next_question (or null if test complete), current_estimate.
acceptance_criteria: Correct answers increase the difficulty estimate by a diminishing step toward the next CEFR level
Incorrect answers decrease the difficulty estimate by a diminishing step toward the previous CEFR level
The test terminates after the configured maximum number of questions (default 10)
The test terminates early if the difficulty estimate stabilizes for 2 consecutive questions
PlacementStepResult includes is_correct, explanation text, the next question (or null), and current_estimate

tasks.title: Implement CEFR level mapping from test score
tasks.description: Implement a finalize_placement(session: PlacementSession) -> PlacementResult method in PlacementTestService. This method calculates the final CEFR level from the test session's difficulty history using a weighted average (later questions weighted more heavily). PlacementResult is a Pydantic schema with: final_level (CEFRLevel), score (float 0.0-1.0), questions_answered (int), correct_count (int), accuracy (float), level_description (str), recommended_starting_topics (list[str]). The method calls ProfileService.complete_placement to persist the result.
acceptance_criteria: Final CEFR level is calculated using a weighted average of the difficulty history (later questions weighted 2x)
PlacementResult includes final_level, numeric score, accuracy percentage, and question statistics
recommended_starting_topics returns 3-5 topic suggestions appropriate for the determined level
The result is persisted via ProfileService.complete_placement (current_level and placement_score updated)
A user scoring 0% accuracy is placed at A1; a user scoring 100% is placed at C2

modules.name: AI Lesson Generator
modules.description: Core lesson generation pipeline: data models, AI-powered content generation with structured output, caching, personalization, API endpoints, scoring, TTS integration, and fault tolerance.

tasks.title: Create Lesson SQLAlchemy model
tasks.description: Define a Lesson model inheriting from Base with IDMixin and TimestampMixin. Columns: language_id (ForeignKey to languages.id, not null), cefr_level (Enum CEFRLevel, not null), topic (String(200), not null), title (String(300), not null), content (JSON, not null — stores the full LessonContent as a JSONB blob), audio_urls (JSON, nullable — stores dict of audio file URLs), generation_model (String(100), nullable — tracks which AI model generated it), generation_duration_ms (Integer, nullable). Add a composite index on (language_id, cefr_level, topic) for cache lookups. Generate an Alembic migration.
acceptance_criteria: Lesson model has all specified columns with correct types and constraints
content column uses JSONB (PostgreSQL) for efficient querying of lesson content
A composite index exists on (language_id, cefr_level, topic) for fast cache lookups
An Alembic migration creates the lessons table with all columns, indexes, and foreign keys
The model tracks generation metadata (model name and generation duration) for analytics

tasks.title: Create UserLesson model
tasks.description: Define a UserLesson model (join table) inheriting from Base with IDMixin and TimestampMixin. Columns: user_id (ForeignKey to users.id, not null), lesson_id (ForeignKey to lessons.id, not null), status (String(20), default 'not_started' — values: not_started, in_progress, completed), score (Float, nullable — 0.0 to 1.0), started_at (DateTime, nullable), completed_at (DateTime, nullable), time_spent_seconds (Integer, default 0), exercises_correct (Integer, default 0), exercises_total (Integer, default 0), xp_earned (Integer, default 0). Add a unique constraint on (user_id, lesson_id). Generate an Alembic migration.
acceptance_criteria: UserLesson links users and lessons with a unique constraint on (user_id, lesson_id)
status defaults to 'not_started' and accepts only defined status values
score is a nullable Float between 0.0 and 1.0
An Alembic migration creates the user_lessons table with all columns and the unique constraint
Deleting a User cascades to delete associated UserLesson records

tasks.title: Implement lesson repository
tasks.description: Create LessonRepository extending AbstractRepository[Lesson, UUID]. Implement all abstract CRUD methods plus: find_cached(language_id: UUID, cefr_level: CEFRLevel, topic: str) -> Lesson | None (cache lookup by the composite index), get_by_level(language_id: UUID, cefr_level: CEFRLevel, limit: int, offset: int) -> list[Lesson], count_by_level(language_id: UUID, cefr_level: CEFRLevel) -> int. Create UserLessonRepository extending AbstractRepository[UserLesson, UUID] with: get_user_lesson(user_id: UUID, lesson_id: UUID) -> UserLesson | None, get_user_history(user_id: UUID, status: str | None, limit: int, offset: int) -> list[UserLesson], get_next_incomplete(user_id: UUID) -> UserLesson | None, update_progress(user_lesson_id: UUID, updates: dict) -> UserLesson.
acceptance_criteria: find_cached queries the composite index on (language_id, cefr_level, topic) and returns Lesson or None
get_by_level returns paginated lessons filtered by language and CEFR level
get_user_history supports optional filtering by status and returns paginated results ordered by started_at desc
get_next_incomplete returns the first UserLesson with status != 'completed' or None
update_progress applies partial updates to a UserLesson record and returns the updated instance

tasks.title: Create lesson generation Pydantic schemas
tasks.description: Define the LessonContent Pydantic model in schemas/lesson.py that represents the full structured output from Vertex AI. Fields: theory (TheoryBlock with title, explanation, key_points list, grammar_notes str), examples (list of ExampleBlock with source_text, translation, context, difficulty), vocabulary (list of VocabItem with word, translation, pronunciation, part_of_speech, example_sentence, audio_url nullable), exercises (list of ExerciseBlock with type enum [multiple_choice, fill_blank, translation, reorder], question, options list nullable, correct_answer, explanation, hints list), test (list of TestQuestion with question, options, correct_index, points int), speaking_task (SpeakingTask with prompt, expected_response_keywords list, difficulty, duration_seconds), reading_text (ReadingBlock with title, content, comprehension_questions list), listening_script (ListeningBlock with script_text, questions list, audio_url nullable). Also define LessonRequest, LessonResponse, and LessonProgressUpdate schemas.
acceptance_criteria: LessonContent contains all eight nested blocks: theory, examples, vocabulary, exercises, test, speaking_task, reading_text, listening_script
Each nested block is a separate Pydantic model with typed fields and validation
ExerciseBlock.type is an enum with values: multiple_choice, fill_blank, translation, reorder
All models have proper field descriptions for OpenAPI documentation generation
LessonContent is usable as a response_schema parameter for VertexAIProvider.generate_structured

tasks.title: Implement LessonGeneratorService with structured output
tasks.description: Create LessonGeneratorService that uses VertexAIProvider.generate_structured to generate complete lesson content. Constructor receives VertexAIProvider, PromptManager, LessonRepository, and ProfileService. Method: generate_lesson(language: Language, level: CEFRLevel, topic: str, user_goals: list[str] | None) -> Lesson. The method: 1) checks the cache via LessonRepository.find_cached, 2) if miss, renders the lesson generation prompt via PromptManager with language, level, topic, and goals, 3) calls generate_structured with response_schema=LessonContent, 4) saves the generated Lesson to the database with content as JSONB, 5) returns the Lesson. Track generation time in generation_duration_ms.
acceptance_criteria: Cache hit: if find_cached returns a Lesson, return it immediately without calling Vertex AI
Cache miss: render prompt, call generate_structured(response_schema=LessonContent), and persist the result
The generated Lesson stores the full LessonContent dict in the content JSONB column
generation_duration_ms records the wall-clock time of the Vertex AI call
generation_model records the model name used (e.g., 'gemini-1.5-pro')

tasks.title: Implement lesson caching strategy
tasks.description: Enhance LessonGeneratorService with a multi-tier caching strategy. Tier 1: database cache via the composite index lookup (already in task-1.3.5). Tier 2: implement a topic normalization layer that canonicalizes topic strings before lookup (lowercase, strip whitespace, collapse synonyms via a configurable mapping). Tier 3: implement a cache invalidation mechanism — lessons older than a configurable LESSON_CACHE_TTL_DAYS (default 30) are considered stale and regenerated. Add a method invalidate_cache(language_id: UUID, level: CEFRLevel, topic: str) -> bool for manual invalidation.
acceptance_criteria: Topic normalization converts 'Past Tense', 'past tense', and '  Past  Tense  ' to the same canonical key
A synonym mapping collapses 'Simple Past' and 'Past Simple' to the same canonical topic
Lessons older than LESSON_CACHE_TTL_DAYS are treated as cache misses and regenerated
invalidate_cache soft-deletes or marks the cached lesson as stale, returning True if found
Cache hit rate is logged at INFO level for monitoring

tasks.title: Implement lesson personalization layer
tasks.description: Add a personalization step to LessonGeneratorService that adapts lesson content to the user's goals and learning context. Before calling Vertex AI, enrich the prompt with: user's primary goal (travel, business, etc.), native language (for translation examples), previous lesson topics (to avoid repetition and build on prior knowledge), weak areas from exercise history (topics where accuracy < 60%). Create a PersonalizationContext Pydantic model that aggregates this data and is passed to the prompt template.
acceptance_criteria: PersonalizationContext includes user_goal, native_language, previous_topics (last 10), and weak_areas
The lesson generation prompt includes personalization context when available
A user with goal='business' receives business-themed examples (prompt includes 'business context' instruction)
Previous topics are excluded from the generated lesson's vocabulary section to avoid redundancy
If personalization data is unavailable (new user), the service generates a generic lesson without error

tasks.title: Create lesson API router
tasks.description: Create an APIRouter in api/routers/lessons.py with prefix /lessons and tag 'Lessons'. Endpoints: GET /lessons/next (generates or retrieves the next lesson for the authenticated user based on their level and a topic selection algorithm, returns LessonResponse, 200), GET /lessons/{lesson_id} (returns a specific lesson by ID, returns LessonResponse, 200), POST /lessons/{lesson_id}/complete (accepts LessonCompletionRequest with exercise results, calculates score, updates UserLesson, returns LessonCompletionResponse, 200), GET /lessons/history (returns paginated list of the user's completed lessons, returns list[LessonSummaryResponse], 200). All endpoints require authentication.
acceptance_criteria: GET /lessons/next returns 200 with a full LessonResponse including all content blocks
GET /lessons/{lesson_id} returns 200 with the lesson or 404 if not found
POST /lessons/{lesson_id}/complete accepts exercise results and returns the calculated score and XP earned
GET /lessons/history returns a paginated list with limit/offset query parameters
All endpoints require Bearer token authentication and return 401 for unauthenticated requests

tasks.title: Implement lesson score calculation and progress tracking
tasks.description: Create a LessonScoringService that calculates the lesson score and awards XP. Method: calculate_score(lesson: Lesson, answers: LessonCompletionRequest) -> ScoringResult. Logic: each exercise and test question has a point value; the score is correct_points / total_points. XP formula: base_xp (50) + score_bonus (score * 100) + streak_bonus (streak_count * 5, capped at 50). The service updates UserLesson (score, completed_at, exercises_correct, exercises_total, xp_earned), ProfileService.add_xp, and ProfileService.update_streak. ScoringResult Pydantic model: score (float), xp_earned (int), exercises_correct (int), exercises_total (int), accuracy (float), level_up (bool — True if enough XP to progress to next CEFR level).
acceptance_criteria: Score is calculated as correct_points / total_points across all exercises and test questions
XP formula includes base_xp (50), score_bonus (score * 100), and streak_bonus (capped at 50)
UserLesson is updated with score, completed_at, exercises_correct, exercises_total, xp_earned, and status='completed'
User's total_xp and streak_count are updated via ProfileService
level_up flag is True when cumulative XP crosses a configurable threshold for the next CEFR level

tasks.title: Implement TTS generation for listening material with Cloud Storage caching
tasks.description: Extend LessonGeneratorService with a post-generation step that converts listening_script.script_text to audio via TextToSpeechService, uploads to Cloud Storage via StorageService, and stores the signed URL in the Lesson's audio_urls JSONB field. Use content-hash-based storage keys (from StorageService) to avoid regenerating audio for identical scripts. Generate audio for vocabulary pronunciations as well — batch TTS requests for all VocabItem entries. Update the Lesson record with populated audio_url fields.
acceptance_criteria: Listening script text is converted to MP3 audio via TextToSpeechService.synthesize_and_store
The audio URL is stored in the Lesson's audio_urls JSONB under key 'listening_audio'
Content-hash storage keys prevent duplicate audio files for identical script text
Vocabulary items receive individual pronunciation audio URLs stored in their audio_url field
If TTS fails for a specific item, the lesson is still returned with audio_url set to null (graceful degradation)

tasks.title: Implement retry logic for partial block failures
tasks.description: Add fault tolerance to LessonGeneratorService for cases where Vertex AI generates incomplete or malformed content. Strategy: 1) Validate the generated LessonContent — if any required block is missing or fails Pydantic validation, retry generation up to MAX_GENERATION_RETRIES (default 3) with an adjusted prompt that explicitly requests the missing blocks. 2) If a block still fails after retries, fill it with a minimal placeholder (e.g., empty exercises list) and log a WARNING. 3) Implement exponential backoff between retries (1s, 2s, 4s). 4) Track failure rates per block type for observability. Create a GenerationReport Pydantic model that accompanies each lesson with: blocks_generated, blocks_failed, retry_count, total_duration_ms.
acceptance_criteria: Incomplete LessonContent triggers up to MAX_GENERATION_RETRIES retry attempts
Retry prompts explicitly request only the missing or invalid blocks
Exponential backoff is applied between retries (configurable base delay)
After max retries, missing blocks are filled with minimal valid placeholders and a WARNING is logged
GenerationReport tracks blocks_generated, blocks_failed, retry_count, and total_duration_ms for each lesson
